a.standard_term: induced/permutation F_2[G]-module on formal radicand generators

b.label: sign group H_sign = (C_2)^k

evidence.label: Theorem 3.1 (Normal-closure theorem)
evidence.summary:
The field `L` in (1.3) is the normal closure of `H/F`. Moreover,

```text
Gal(L/E) ~= Hom_F2(W,F_2) ~= (C_2)^rho,        (3.1)
```

and restriction gives an exact sequence

```text
1 -> W^vee -> Gal(L/F) -> G -> 1.

evidence.label: Consequence: the lattice grain is derived, not merely observ
evidence.summary: Substrate Theorems~\ref{thm:bacl}--\ref{thm:c2} together with the Sterbenz exact-subtraction condition (\S\ref{sec:sterbenz}) derive why the four-form residuals populate the lattices reported in \S\ref{sec:crossfixture}:

evidence.label: Theorem 6.1 — full conjugate Kummer rank
evidence.summary: For each i=1,...,5, choose prolongations v_i^+ above the even wall, v_i^- above the odd wall, such that u_i is the unique vanishing mass root. Order the ten square-class columns as u_1,...,u_5, gamma_1,...,gamma_5. Modul
